skill.name: code-review-ai-ai-review
skill.description: AI-powered code review specialist that combines automated static analysis tools with AI-assisted contextual understanding to identify bugs, vulnerabilities, and performance issues across 30+ languages.
skill.summary: Automated code review skill combining SonarQube, CodeQL, Semgrep with Claude/GPT for comprehensive security, performance, and architecture analysis.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 53 static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate code review assistant that integrates security scanning tools (SonarQube, CodeQL, Semgrep, TruffleHog) with AI models. External commands, environment access, and network calls are all required for its core function of automated code analysis and GitHub integration.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution
low_findings.description: The skill contains examples of running static analysis tools (sonar-scanner, semgrep, codeql) via subprocess. These are hardcoded tool invocations required for code review functionality - not user input injection vectors.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Subprocess calls are hardcoded with tool names, repository variable is internal constructor input, not untrusted user data

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access
low_findings.description: Accesses GITHUB_TOKEN and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variables. These are required for authenticating with GitHub API to post review comments and Claude API for AI analysis.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard API authentication pattern for legitimate GitHub and Claude integration

low_findings.title: Network Request to External URL
low_findings.description: Contains a reference URL to cwe.mitre.org for vulnerability documentation. This is a documentation link, not a data exfiltration endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static reference URL for Common Weakness Enumeration documentation

low_findings.title: File System Operations
low_findings.description: Reads review-comments.json file to post comments via GitHub API. Standard file I/O for workflow automation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Local file read for reading generated review data, not arbitrary file access

low_findings.title: Secret Detection Tools
low_findings.description: Shows integration with TruffleHog for secret scanning. This is a defensive security tool to DETECT leaked secrets, not to exfiltrate them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: TruffleHog is a well-known open source secret detection tool used for defensive security

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm References
low_findings.description: Mentions MD5 and SHA-1 in OWASP Top 10 context as vulnerabilities to DETECT (e.g., weak password hashing), not as algorithms the skill uses.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation of security vulnerabilities to detect, not implementation of weak crypto

dangerous_patterns.title: Code Execution + Network + Credentials Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The heuristic flagged the combination of code execution, network access, and credential access. In this context, this is a LEGITIMATE pattern: running security scanning tools (subprocess), calling AI APIs (network), and authenticating via tokens (credentials). This is the expected architecture for an AI code review workflow.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Required combination for automated code review: run tools, call AI APIs, post results to GitHub

content.value_statement: Transform manual code review into automated AI-assisted quality assurance. This skill combines static analysis tools with Claude and GPT models to catch security vulnerabilities, performance issues, and architecture problems early.
actual_capabilities: Run automated static analysis using SonarQube, CodeQL, and Semgrep
Detect security vulnerabilities following OWASP Top 10 guidelines
Identify performance issues like N+1 queries and missing indexes
Generate structured review comments with severity levels and fix examples
Integrate with GitHub Actions for automated PR reviews
Scan for leaked secrets using TruffleHog and GitGuardian
limitations: Does not execute code - only analyzes static patterns
May produce false positives requiring human verification
Requires API keys (GitHub, Claude/OpenAI) for full functionality
Language support depends on static analysis tool capabilities

use_cases.title: Automated Pull Request Reviews
use_cases.description: Integrate with CI/CD pipelines to automatically review every pull request, posting structured comments with security, performance, and architecture feedback.
use_cases.target_user: Development teams wanting to scale code review coverage

use_cases.title: Security-Focused Audits
use_cases.description: Run comprehensive security analysis using CodeQL and Semgrep to identify SQL injection, XSS, auth bypasses, and other critical vulnerabilities.

use_cases.title: Performance Optimization
use_cases.description: Detect common performance anti-patterns like N+1 queries, missing database indexes, and unbounded collections before they reach production.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Code Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick review of a small PR with focused feedback

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Security Scan
prompt_templates.prompt:
Perform a deep security analysis of this code change. Check for:
1. SQL injection and command injection vulnerabilities
2. Authentication and authorization flaws
3. Insecure cryptographic practices
4. Data exposure risks

Code:
{code_snippet}

Static analysis results:
{static_results}
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused security review with static analysis data

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Review
prompt_templates.prompt:
Analyze this code change for architectural concerns:
- Does it follow SOLID principles?
- Are dependencies properly managed?
- Is there proper separation of concerns?
- Any potential scalability issues?

Code:
{code}

System context: {architecture_summary}
prompt_templates.scenario: Review of architectural decisions and design patterns

prompt_templates.title: Full Stack Review with CI Integration
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete automated review matching production CI workflows

output:
## Security Review Findings

**CRITICAL - SQL Injection**
- File: `src/auth/login.ts:42`
- String concatenation with user input enables SQL injection
- Fix: Use parameterized queries

**HIGH - Weak Password Storage**
- File: `src/auth/user.ts:15`
- Using MD5 for password hashing
- Fix: Use bcrypt or Argon2

output_examples.input: Analyze this database query for performance issues
output:
## Performance Analysis

**HIGH - N+1 Query Detected**
- File: `src/api/users.js:28`
- Loop contains 5 database calls
- Impact: 100 users = 500 queries
- Fix: Use JOIN or batch loading

## Architecture Review

**WARNING - Shared Database**
- Service boundaries violated
- Fix: Implement database-per-service pattern

**INFO - Missing Circuit Breaker**
- External API calls lack resilience
- Recommendation: Add circuit breaker pattern

best_practices: Run static analysis tools (CodeQL, Semgrep) before AI analysis to provide contextual data
Use temperature=0.1-0.2 for consistent, deterministic security reviews
Set quality gates that block PRs with CRITICAL severity findings
anti_patterns: Relying solely on AI without static analysis context - AI can miss known vulnerability patterns
Setting temperature too high (>0.5) leading to inconsistent or fabricated findings
Ignoring false positive rates - always verify critical findings manually

faq.question: What static analysis tools does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill integrates with SonarQube, CodeQL, Semgrep, TruffleHog, and GitGuardian for comprehensive security scanning.

faq.question: Which AI models work best for code review?
faq.answer: Claude 4.5 Sonnet and GPT-4o are recommended for detailed analysis. For quick reviews under 200 lines, Haiku or mini models suffice.

faq.question: Do I need API keys to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need GITHUB_TOKEN for posting comments and either ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY for AI analysis.

faq.question: Can this skill detect secrets in code?
faq.answer: Yes, it includes TruffleHog integration for detecting leaked API keys, passwords, and other sensitive credentials.

faq.question: How does the CI/CD integration work?
faq.answer: The skill provides GitHub Actions workflow examples that run static analysis, call AI APIs, and post structured review comments.

faq.question: What languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports 30+ languages through CodeQL and Semgrep language-specific rules.
